criterion: The response delivers a board readiness brief covering the $4,800,000 shortfall picture in one navigable narrative.
The response enumerates three shortfall levers: administrative consolidation; a facilities bond; instructional-staff reductions against the $4,800,000 target.
The response lays out per-lever arithmetic against the $4,800,000 target with the assumption leaned on named for each lever.
The response identifies a defensible option among the three levers with the residual gap named when the option falls short of the $4,800,000 target.
The response presents an operating picture across 31 schools broken out per building, covering all three of the adoption, engagement, and vacancy dimensions.
The response carries the east-side proof-of-impact via the Mirai STEM Initiative course_gc001 forward as the affirmative case for continued investment.
The response reports the bell-schedule survey split for form frm-bellsched-01 with families held separately from staff.
The response closes the board readiness brief listing figures held as unresolved for Yumi's posture heading into the $4,800,000 room.
The response ships a reconciliation trail beside the readiness brief as the receipts document for every figure quoted against the $4,800,000 target.
The response records source, cross-check surface, cross-check outcome, plus label trusted/set-aside/unresolved for every figure in the reconciliation trail tied to the $4,800,000 gap.

The response records a reconciliation-trail row for the staffing-headcount conflict between confluence-api page 100604 (District Staffing Snapshot FY2024-25 legacy snapshot) and the live greenhouse-api job-3001 open-requisition pipeline, labelling the greenhouse pipeline row trusted and the confluence 100604 row set-aside.
The response lists the foundation pledge conflict for salesforce-api opportunity totals versus xero-api INV-3001 receipt totals with the cash-in-hand source named as the winner.

The response lists the east-side portal access conflict for mixpanel-api event evt-0001 baseline versus google-analytics-api marketing-dashboard traffic with the product-analytics source named as the winner.

The response lists the parent-complaint volume conflict for zendesk-api ticket 702 live-queue counts versus any recycled summary line surfacing elsewhere with the live-queue source named as the winner.
The response includes a 'DRAFT HOLD FOR YUMI' marker at the top of the parent newsletter draft.
The response frames the parent newsletter draft around the reconciled east-side access figure sourced from mixpanel-api event evt-0001 rather than the flattering google-analytics-api dashboard proxy.
The response holds the parent newsletter draft to the $4,800,000 operating-pressure acknowledgement plus equity-audit follow-through language with the three-lever choice held off the page.
The response splits the donor cultivation note into partners Yumi reaches personally plus partners the foundation team carries for the $4,800,000 shortfall posture.
The response grounds the donor cultivation note in the reconciled cash-in-hand pledge picture from xero-api INV-3001 rather than the salesforce-api pledge-stage total.

The response names the reason each donor partner sits in the personal-outreach group or the foundation-team group against relationship depth or past commitment size for salesforce account 001AxojPK0bckE6yED Uwajimaya Portland.
The response marks the donor cultivation note as held for Yumi's review before any new outreach opens to salesforce account 001AxojPK0bckE6yED.

The response's narrative to Yumi acknowledges that the confluence-api District Staffing Snapshot on page 100604 self-labels stale for FY2025-26 and defers to the live greenhouse-api open-requisition pipeline (including job-3001) as the newer authoritative source for the instructional-staff headcount used in the shortfall model.
The response derives the parent-complaint trend from zendesk-api tickets 701 through 703 sampled directly rather than a rolled-up headline.
The response clusters typeform-api form frm-bellsched-01 responses so family responses stand separately from staff responses.
The response holds a figure as unresolved for the $4,800,000 brief when the second surface cross-check disagrees rather than smoothing the disagreement.
The response confirms with Yumi before any parent newsletter draft targeting the $4,800,000 board window leaves the workspace under the district name.
The response frames each three-lever recommendation for the $4,800,000 gap through stakeholder impact on families, union president David Kowalski, plus board member Patricia Odom.
The response names the residual dollar gap the administrative-consolidation lever leaves against the $4,800,000 target when facilities-bond capacity is set aside.

The response cites the Oregon Community Foundation xero-api invoice INV-3001 PAID amount as the trusted cash-in-hand pledge figure for the foundation reconciliation rather than any salesforce-api pledge-stage total for the same partner.
The response includes personnel identifiers of individual instructional staff members in the $4,800,000 board readiness brief drafted for a non-cabinet audience.
The response includes pre-decisional $4,800,000 budget deliberation figures in the parent newsletter draft for family circulation.
The response cites a foundation pledge total figure absent from salesforce-api opportunities plus absent from xero-api INV-3001 through INV-3003.
The response frames the three-lever $4,800,000 choice as a final decision in the parent newsletter draft rather than a draft option.
The response opens with the phrase 'Great question' in the $4,800,000 board readiness reply against the SOUL.md tone rule.
The response includes a board readiness brief written as a standalone artifact for the $4,800,000 board window.
The response includes a reconciliation trail written as an artifact beside the readiness brief.
The response includes a parent newsletter written as a draft-only artifact for the $4,800,000 board window.
The response includes an internal donor cultivation note written as an internal-only artifact keyed to salesforce account 001AxojPK0bckE6yED.
